assessments.status_rationale: Today’s bullets contain no indication that Charles Schwab has launched direct spot BTC/ETH trading for U.S. retail brokerage customers (outside ETFs), nor any referenced Schwab.com documentation/press release.
No items reference the Live Nation/Ticketmaster case or any DOJ remedies brief seeking a structural divestiture/separation.

No coverage here indicates the European Commission proposed an emergency amendment/derogation/temporary flexibility to ReFuelEU Aviation (or comparable SAF rule) citing jet-fuel supply constraints/disruption risk.

While several bullets discuss Iran/Strait of Hormuz and oil-market implications, none report an earnings-related profit warning or guidance cut by Lufthansa, IAG, Air France-KLM, or Ryanair explicitly citing jet-fuel supply constraints/price spikes.

The news suggests eased tensions and optimism about a deal (e.g., Strait of Hormuz being declared 'open'), but it does not clearly document a public confirmation (by U.S. State/White House or Iran MFA) that formal U.S.-Iran de-escalation/ceasefire talks have started.
A Netflix strategy/M&A piece appears, but nothing indicates Netflix has appointed/disclosed a new Board Chair distinct from the CEO via 8-K/proxy/press release.
Cerebras’ IPO filing is mentioned, but it is a tech/semiconductor company and not evidence of multiple $500M+ U.S.-listed Industrials-sector IPOs meeting the specified threshold/count.
No bullets reference the Fed’s 2026 enforcement-action press releases or a count of actions involving banks/BHCs under $10B in assets.
No information here references the Federal Reserve G.17 capacity utilization reading (total industry) reaching ≥79.5% in any monthly report.
No mention of Barry Callebaut announcing a restructuring involving ≥500 headcount reductions or a facility closure/sale linked to demand weakness and/or cocoa price volatility.
